title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to increase the employer tax credit for paid family and medical leave.
plain_summary: This bill changes tax rules to help employers who give their workers paid time off for family reasons or medical problems. Right now, employers can get some money back on their taxes when they offer this benefit, but the bill would double that amount. The bill also makes this tax break permanent so employers can count on it every year instead of it ending after a certain time. These changes would start in 2026 and are meant to encourage more companies to offer paid family and medical leave to their employees.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1  Increase in Employer Credit for Paid Family and Medical Leave
sections.text: This section changes the tax credit that employers can get when they give paid time off to workers for family and medical reasons. First, it doubles the credit percentages. Where the law used to give employers twelve and a half percent credit, it now gives twenty-five percent. Where it used to give twenty-five percent, it now gives fifty percent. The increases also go up by half a percentage point instead of a quarter percentage point. Second, this section makes the credit permanent by removing the part of the tax code that had an end date for this benefit. These changes will start applying to tax years that begin after December thirty-first, two thousand twenty-five.
